endometrial cancer.symptoms: Abnormal vaginal bleeding, pelvic pain or discomfort, changes in bowel or bladder habits
endometrial cancer.treatments: Surgery (hysterectomy), radiation therapy, chemotherapy, hormone therapy

cystic fibrosis.symptoms: Persistent cough with thick mucus, recurrent lung infections, poor growth or weight gain, salty-tasting skin
cystic fibrosis.treatments: Medications (mucus-thinning drugs, bronchodilators), chest physical therapy, enzyme supplements, lung transplant (in severe cases)

intertrigo.symptoms: Red, raw, or inflamed skin in skin folds (such as armpits or groin), itching or burning sensation
intertrigo.treatments: Keeping the affected area clean and dry, applying topical antifungal or antibacterial creams, using barrier creams

parathyroid adenoma.symptoms: Fatigue, weakness, bone pain, frequent urination, kidney stones
parathyroid adenoma.treatments: Surgical removal of the adenoma (parathyroidectomy), medications to manage calcium levels

glucocorticoid deficiency.symptoms: Fatigue, weakness, weight loss, low blood pressure, abdominal pain
glucocorticoid deficiency.treatments: Hormone replacement therapy with glucocorticoid medications

temporomandibular joint disorder.symptoms: Jaw pain or tenderness, clicking or popping sounds in the jaw joint, difficulty chewing or opening the mouth
temporomandibular joint disorder.treatments: Self-care measures (rest, applying heat or cold, jaw exercises), pain medications, physical therapy, oral splints

wilson disease.symptoms: Fatigue, abdominal pain or swelling, jaundice, neurological symptoms (tremors, difficulty speaking or walking)
wilson disease.treatments: Medications to remove copper from the body (chelators), dietary changes, liver transplant (in severe cases)

vesicoureteral reflux.symptoms: Frequent urinary tract infections, urinary urgency or frequency, bedwetting (in children), high blood pressure (in severe cases)
vesicoureteral reflux.treatments: Antibiotics, bladder training exercises, surgical correction (in some cases)

vitamin a deficiency.symptoms: Night blindness, dry eyes, dry or rough skin, poor wound healing, weakened immune system
vitamin a deficiency.treatments: Vitamin A supplementation, dietary changes to include foods rich in vitamin A

gonorrhea.symptoms: Painful urination, abnormal discharge from the genitals, pelvic pain
gonorrhea.treatments: Antibiotics (dual therapy with ceftriaxone and azithromycin), safe sexual practices, partner notification

fracture of the rib.symptoms: Chest pain, difficulty breathing, tenderness or swelling over the ribcage
fracture of the rib.treatments: Pain management, rest, immobilization (splinting or bracing), physical therapy

ependymoma.symptoms: Headaches, nausea, seizures, changes in coordination or balance
ependymoma.treatments: Surgery, radiation therapy, chemotherapy, targeted therapy, rehabilitation

hepatitis due to a toxin.symptoms: Fatigue, jaundice, abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting
hepatitis due to a toxin.treatments: Supportive care, removal or avoidance of the toxic substance, management of symptoms

vaginal cyst.symptoms: Small lump or swelling in the vaginal wall, discomfort or pain during intercourse
vaginal cyst.treatments: Observation (if asymptomatic), warm compresses, sitz baths, surgical removal (if necessary)

chronic knee pain.symptoms: Persistent knee pain, stiffness, swelling, limited range of motion
chronic knee pain.treatments: Pain management, physical therapy, lifestyle modifications, knee braces or supports

pinguecula.symptoms: Yellowish or white growth on the conjunctiva (eye's surface), usually near the cornea
pinguecula.treatments: Artificial tears, lubricating eye drops, sunglasses for UV protection, surgical removal (in severe cases)

hypergammaglobulinemia.symptoms: Elevated levels of gamma globulins in the blood, may be asymptomatic or associated with other underlying conditions
hypergammaglobulinemia.treatments: Treatment of underlying cause or associated condition, immunosuppressive therapy (in some cases)

pituitary disorder.symptoms: Various symptoms depending on the specific disorder, including hormonal imbalances
pituitary disorder.treatments: Treatment depends on the specific pituitary disorder and may involve medications, surgery, hormone replacement therapy

kidney stone.symptoms: Severe pain in the side or back, blood in the urine, frequent urination
kidney stone.treatments: Pain management, increased fluid intake, medication to facilitate stone passage, minimally invasive procedures or surgery

autism.symptoms: Challenges with social interaction, communication difficulties, restricted interests or repetitive behaviors
autism.treatments: Behavioral therapy, educational interventions, speech and language therapy, medications for associated symptoms

cat scratch disease.symptoms: Swollen lymph nodes, fever, fatigue, headache, rash
cat scratch disease.treatments: Antibiotics (azithromycin, doxycycline), pain management, supportive care

chronic glaucoma.symptoms: Gradual vision loss, peripheral vision impairment, eye pain or discomfort, halos around lights
chronic glaucoma.treatments: Eye drops (to reduce intraocular pressure), oral medications, laser therapy, surgery (trabeculectomy, implantable devices), regular eye exams

retinal detachment.symptoms: Floaters (spots or cobweb-like shapes in vision), flashes of light, curtain-like shadow over vision, sudden vision loss
retinal detachment.treatments: Surgical intervention (pneumatic retinopexy, scleral buckle, vitrectomy), laser therapy, cryotherapy, gas or silicone oil injection, positioning, regular eye exams

aplastic anemia.symptoms: Fatigue, pale skin, frequent infections, shortness of breath, rapid or irregular heartbeats, prolonged bleeding or bruising
aplastic anemia.treatments: Blood transfusions, bone marrow-stimulating medications, immunosuppressive therapy, stem cell transplant (in severe cases), supportive care

overflow incontinence.symptoms: Frequent or constant dribbling of urine, inability to completely empty the bladder, weak urine stream
overflow incontinence.treatments: Bladder training exercises, double voiding, catheterization, medications (alpha-blockers, anticholinergics), surgery (in severe cases), lifestyle modifications

rabies.symptoms: Flu-like symptoms (fever, headache, fatigue), anxiety, confusion, hallucinations, difficulty swallowing
rabies.treatments: Immediate medical attention (post-exposure prophylaxis), wound care, administration of rabies vaccine and immunoglobulin, supportive care, monitoring and management of symptoms

hemolytic anemia.symptoms: Fatigue, weakness, pale skin, shortness of breath, jaundice
hemolytic anemia.treatments: Treatment of underlying cause, blood transfusions, medications (immunosuppressants, corticosteroids), splenectomy (in some cases), supportive care, folic acid or iron supplementation

lateral epicondylitis (tennis elbow).symptoms: Pain or tenderness on the outer side of the elbow, weak grip strength, difficulty with forearm movements
lateral epicondylitis (tennis elbow).treatments: Rest, ice or cold therapy, compression, elbow brace or strap, pain relievers, physical therapy exercises, corticosteroid injections (in severe cases), ergonomic modifications

syphilis.symptoms: Primary stage: painless sores (chancre) at the site of infection, secondary stage: skin rash, fever, swollen lymph nodes, sore throat, latent stage: no symptoms, tertiary stage: severe complications affecting the heart, brain, and other organs
syphilis.treatments: Antibiotics (usually penicillin), treatment of complications, regular follow-up and testing

diabetic kidney disease.symptoms: High blood pressure, increased need to urinate, swelling in the legs and ankles, fatigue, nausea, loss of appetite
diabetic kidney disease.treatments: Blood pressure control, blood sugar control, medications to protect the kidneys (such as ACE inhibitors or ARBs), lifestyle modifications (healthy diet, regular exercise, quitting smoking), management of underlying diabetes, treatment of complications (such as dialysis or kidney transplantation in advanced stages)

nose disorder.symptoms: Nasal congestion, runny nose, sneezing, facial pain or pressure, loss of smell
nose disorder.treatments: Treatment depends on the underlying cause (such as allergies, sinusitis, or nasal polyps), and may include medications (such as decongestants, antihistamines, nasal sprays), saline nasal irrigation, steam inhalation, allergy management, antibiotics (if bacterial infection is present), surgical intervention (in some cases)

drug withdrawal.symptoms: Anxiety, restlessness, irritability, tremors, sweating, nausea, vomiting, insomnia, drug cravings
drug withdrawal.treatments: Gradual tapering of the drug under medical supervision, medications to manage withdrawal symptoms (such as benzodiazepines, clonidine, or buprenorphine), behavioral therapy, support groups, counseling, rehabilitation programs

dental caries.symptoms: Toothache, tooth sensitivity, visible pits or holes in the teeth, dark spots on the teeth, bad breath
dental caries.treatments: Dental fillings, crowns, root canal treatment, tooth extraction (in severe cases), oral hygiene practices (brushing and flossing), regular dental check-ups, fluoride treatments, dietary modifications (reducing sugary and acidic foods and drinks), dental sealants (for prevention), education on proper oral care

hypercholesterolemia.symptoms: High levels of cholesterol in the blood, fatty deposits (xanthomas) on the skin or tendons, chest pain or angina, heart attacks, strokes
hypercholesterolemia.treatments: Lifestyle modifications (healthy diet, regular exercise, weight management, smoking cessation), medications to lower cholesterol levels (such as statins), regular monitoring of cholesterol levels, management of underlying conditions (such as diabetes or high blood pressure), potential additional treatments (such as bile acid sequestrants or PCSK9 inhibitors)

fracture of the patella.symptoms: Severe pain, swelling, bruising, difficulty straightening or bending the knee, deformity
fracture of the patella.treatments: Immobilization (splint, cast, or brace), pain management, physical therapy, exercises to restore mobility and strength, potential surgical intervention (in severe cases or if displacement is present)

kidney failure.symptoms: Decreased urine output, fluid retention, fatigue, shortness of breath, nausea, confusion, chest pain, seizures
kidney failure.treatments: Treatment depends on the underlying cause and may include medications to manage blood pressure and symptoms, dietary modifications (restricting salt, protein, and potassium), dialysis (hemodialysis or peritoneal dialysis) or kidney transplantation (in end-stage kidney failure), treatment of complications, management of fluid and electrolyte balance, lifestyle modifications (exercise, quitting smoking, healthy diet)

fracture of the neck.symptoms: Severe neck pain, swelling, tenderness, difficulty moving or turning the head, numbness or weakness in the arms or legs
fracture of the neck.treatments: Immobilization (cervical collar, brace), pain management, potential surgical intervention (in severe cases)

muscle spasm.symptoms: Sudden, involuntary muscle contractions, muscle pain, stiffness, limited range of motion
muscle spasm.treatments: Stretching and relaxation exercises, heat or cold therapy, over-the-counter pain relievers, muscle relaxants, physical therapy, lifestyle modifications

hemophilia.symptoms: Excessive bleeding and bruising, prolonged bleeding from wounds or after dental work or surgery, spontaneous bleeding in joints or muscles
hemophilia.treatments: Replacement therapy with clotting factor concentrates, medications to control bleeding, physical therapy, joint protection measures, genetic counseling and testing for family members

hyperosmotic hyperketotic state.symptoms: Increased blood sugar levels, increased ketone production, excessive thirst, frequent urination, fatigue, confusion, fruity breath odor
hyperosmotic hyperketotic state.treatments: Insulin therapy to regulate blood sugar levels, intravenous fluids to correct dehydration, electrolyte replacement, correction of underlying causes (e.g., diabetes management)

peritonsillar abscess.symptoms: Severe sore throat, difficulty swallowing, fever, swollen tonsils, voice changes, neck pain or stiffness
peritonsillar abscess.treatments: Antibiotics to treat infection, drainage of the abscess (needle aspiration or incision and drainage), pain relievers, warm saltwater gargles, rest, fluids, surgical removal of the tonsils (in chronic or recurrent cases)

gastroparesis.symptoms: Nausea, vomiting, early satiety, bloating, abdominal pain, poor appetite
gastroparesis.treatments: Dietary modifications (small, frequent meals; low-fiber, low-fat diet), medication to promote gastric emptying, managing underlying cause if possible

fat embolism.symptoms: Respiratory distress, chest pain, confusion, petechial rash (small red or purple spots), neurological symptoms
fat embolism.treatments: Supportive care (oxygen therapy, fluid resuscitation), managing symptoms (pain medication, anticoagulants), treatment of underlying cause if possible

polycythemia vera.symptoms: Increased red blood cell count, fatigue, headache, dizziness, itching, enlarged spleen
polycythemia vera.treatments: Phlebotomy (removal of blood), medication (hydroxyurea, interferon), low-dose aspirin, managing complications (blood thinners, medication for itching)

thrombocytopenia.symptoms: Easy bruising, prolonged bleeding from cuts, petechiae (red or purple spots on the skin), nosebleeds, excessive menstrual bleeding
thrombocytopenia.treatments: Treatment of underlying cause, platelet transfusion (if severe), medication (corticosteroids, immunosuppressants), avoiding medications that can worsen platelet counts

head and neck cancer.symptoms: Persistent sore throat, difficulty swallowing, voice changes, ear pain, neck mass, weight loss
head and neck cancer.treatments: Treatment depends on the specific type and stage of cancer, and may include surgery, radiation therapy, chemotherapy, targeted therapy, immunotherapy

pseudohypoparathyroidism.symptoms: Hypocalcemia (low calcium levels), tetany (muscle spasms), short stature, rounded face
pseudohypoparathyroidism.treatments: Calcium and vitamin D supplementation, management of complications, hormone replacement therapy (if necessary)

goiter.symptoms: Enlarged thyroid gland in the neck, swelling or visible protrusion, difficulty swallowing or breathing
goiter.treatments: Treatment depends on the underlying cause (medication, radioactive iodine therapy, surgery)

urge incontinence.symptoms: Sudden, intense urge to urinate followed by involuntary urine leakage
urge incontinence.treatments: Behavioral therapies (bladder training, pelvic floor exercises), medications (anticholinergics, beta-3 agonists), medical devices

edward syndrome.symptoms: Developmental delays, intellectual disability, distinct facial features (small jaw, low-set ears), heart defects
edward syndrome.treatments: Supportive care, addressing associated medical issues, early intervention programs, palliative care

muscular dystrophy.symptoms: Progressive muscle weakness and degeneration, difficulty walking or moving, muscle wasting
muscular dystrophy.treatments: Supportive care, physical therapy, assistive devices, medications for symptom management, gene therapies (in some cases)

mittelschmerz.symptoms: Lower abdominal pain and discomfort occurring around the time of ovulation
mittelschmerz.treatments: Pain management (over-the-counter pain relievers, heat therapy), hormonal birth control (if severe or recurrent)

corneal abrasion.symptoms: Eye pain, redness, foreign body sensation, sensitivity to light
corneal abrasion.treatments: Eye patching, lubricating eye drops, antibiotic ointments (if infected), avoiding further eye irritation

anemia of chronic disease.symptoms: Fatigue, weakness, pale skin, shortness of breath, decreased exercise tolerance
anemia of chronic disease.treatments: Treating the underlying chronic disease, addressing nutritional deficiencies, blood transfusions (in severe cases)

dysthymic disorder.symptoms: Persistent depressive symptoms (low mood, lack of interest, changes in sleep and appetite), lasting for at least two years
dysthymic disorder.treatments: Psychotherapy (cognitive-behavioral therapy, interpersonal therapy), antidepressant medications

scarlet fever.symptoms: Sore throat, fever, rash (red, sandpaper-like), swollen glands
scarlet fever.treatments: Antibiotics (such as penicillin or erythromycin), rest, fluids, symptomatic relief

hypertensive heart disease.symptoms: High blood pressure, chest pain or discomfort, shortness of breath, fatigue
hypertensive heart disease.treatments: Medications (antihypertensives), lifestyle changes (healthy diet, exercise, stress management), treatment of underlying conditions

drug abuse (barbiturates).symptoms: Sedation, drowsiness, slurred speech, confusion, respiratory depression
drug abuse (barbiturates).treatments: Medical detoxification, supportive care, counseling, rehabilitation programs

polycystic ovarian syndrome.symptoms: Irregular periods, excessive hair growth, acne, weight gain, ovarian cysts
polycystic ovarian syndrome.treatments: Lifestyle changes (diet, exercise, weight management), hormonal contraceptives, fertility treatments (if needed)

encephalitis.symptoms: Headache, fever, confusion, seizures, neurological symptoms
encephalitis.treatments: Hospitalization, antiviral medications (if caused by a viral infection), supportive care

cyst of the eyelid.symptoms: Painful lump or swelling on the eyelid, redness, tenderness
cyst of the eyelid.treatments: Warm compresses, good eyelid hygiene, antibiotic ointments (if infected), surgical removal (if necessary)

balanitis.symptoms: Inflammation of the glans penis (tip of the penis), redness, swelling, pain
balanitis.treatments: Good hygiene, topical antifungal or antibacterial creams, corticosteroid creams (if severe), treating underlying causes

foreign body in the throat.symptoms: Sensation of a foreign object stuck in the throat, difficulty swallowing or speaking
foreign body in the throat.treatments: Immediate medical attention, removal of the foreign body, evaluation for any complications

drug abuse (cocaine).symptoms: Euphoria, increased energy, dilated pupils, rapid heart rate, high blood pressure, risk of heart attack or stroke
drug abuse (cocaine).treatments: Medical detoxification, counseling, behavioral therapies, support groups

optic neuritis.symptoms: Vision loss or blurry vision, eye pain with eye movement, color vision impairment, flashing lights or floaters
optic neuritis.treatments: High-dose intravenous corticosteroids, treatment of underlying cause, symptom management, visual rehabilitation

intestinal malabsorption.symptoms: Chronic diarrhea, weight loss, abdominal pain or bloating, nutrient deficiencies (vitamins, minerals), fatigue, failure to thrive
intestinal malabsorption.treatments: Dietary modifications (specific to the underlying cause), supplementation of deficient nutrients, medications (to manage symptoms or underlying condition), treatment of underlying cause

lead poisoning.symptoms: Fatigue, abdominal pain, joint pain, memory loss or cognitive difficulties, developmental delays (in children), anemia
lead poisoning.treatments: Removal of lead source, chelation therapy (in severe cases), supportive care, medication to manage symptoms, addressing complications (such as anemia)

viral warts.symptoms: Small, rough growths on the skin or mucous membranes, typically painless, may appear in clusters or as single lesions
viral warts.treatments: Topical treatments (salicylic acid, cryotherapy), laser therapy, surgical removal, immune therapy (for persistent or recurrent cases)

hyperhidrosis.symptoms: Excessive sweating (beyond what is necessary for temperature regulation), typically in specific areas (such as palms, soles, underarms), may interfere with daily activities
hyperhidrosis.treatments: Antiperspirants, medications (anticholinergics, beta blockers), iontophoresis, Botox injections, surgical interventions (sympathectomy, sweat gland removal)

stroke.symptoms: Sudden numbness or weakness of the face, arm, or leg (typically on one side of the body), confusion, trouble speaking or understanding, severe headache
stroke.treatments: Emergency medical attention, medication (clot-busting drugs, anticoagulants), supportive care, rehabilitation (physical therapy, speech therapy), lifestyle modifications (diet, exercise, smoking cessation), addressing underlying risk factors

pilonidal cyst.symptoms: Pain or discomfort in the tailbone area, swelling, redness, drainage of pus or blood, abscess formation
pilonidal cyst.treatments: Warm compresses, pain management, antibiotics (for infection), incision and drainage, surgical removal or excision, wound care, lifestyle modifications to prevent recurrence

crushing injury.symptoms: Severe pain, swelling, deformity, bruising, difficulty moving or using the affected area
crushing injury.treatments: Emergency medical attention, immobilization, pain management, surgery (to repair or reconstruct affected tissues), rehabilitation (physical therapy, occupational therapy)

normal pressure hydrocephalus.symptoms: Gait disturbances, urinary incontinence, cognitive decline or dementia-like symptoms
normal pressure hydrocephalus.treatments: Ventriculoperitoneal shunt placement, medication management for symptoms

alopecia.symptoms: Hair loss or balding, patchy or generalized hair loss
alopecia.treatments: Topical medications (minoxidil), oral medications (finasteride), steroid injections, hair transplant surgery, wigs or hairpieces

hashimoto thyroiditis.symptoms: Fatigue, weight gain, constipation, dry skin, depression, muscle aches and stiffness, sensitivity to cold
hashimoto thyroiditis.treatments: Thyroid hormone replacement (levothyroxine), monitoring and management of symptoms

flat feet.symptoms: Foot pain or achiness, difficulty with foot and ankle movement, foot fatigue, swelling
flat feet.treatments: Supportive footwear, orthotic inserts, physical therapy exercises, pain management, surgery (in severe cases)

nonalcoholic liver disease (nash).symptoms: Fatigue, abdominal swelling or pain, enlarged liver, elevated liver enzymes, insulin resistance
nonalcoholic liver disease (nash).treatments: Lifestyle changes (weight loss, healthy diet, regular exercise), management of underlying conditions, medication as needed

hemarthrosis.symptoms: Joint swelling, pain, warmth, limited range of motion
hemarthrosis.treatments: RICE method (Rest, Ice, Compression, Elevation), pain management, joint aspiration or drainage (in severe cases)

pelvic organ prolapse.symptoms: Pelvic pressure or heaviness, urinary incontinence, bowel movement difficulties, discomfort or pain during intercourse
pelvic organ prolapse.treatments: Pelvic floor exercises, pessary use, hormone therapy, surgical repair (in severe cases)

fracture of the arm.symptoms: Pain, swelling, deformity, limited mobility or difficulty moving the arm, bruising
fracture of the arm.treatments: Immobilization (cast or splint), pain management, physical therapy, surgery (in complex fractures)

coagulation (bleeding) disorder.symptoms: Easy bruising, prolonged bleeding after injury or surgery, excessive bleeding from minor cuts or abrasions, frequent nosebleeds, heavy menstrual bleeding
coagulation (bleeding) disorder.treatments: Blood transfusions, clotting factor replacement therapy, medications to stimulate clotting, lifestyle modifications, surgery (in some cases)

intracranial hemorrhage.symptoms: Severe headache, nausea or vomiting, changes in vision, loss of consciousness, seizures
intracranial hemorrhage.treatments: Emergency medical attention, surgery (if needed), medications to control bleeding and manage symptoms

hyperkalemia.symptoms: Muscle weakness or fatigue, irregular heartbeat or palpitations, numbness or tingling, nausea or vomiting
hyperkalemia.treatments: Treatment of underlying cause, medications to reduce potassium levels, dietary modifications, dialysis (in severe cases)

cornea infection.symptoms: Eye redness, pain or discomfort, blurred or decreased vision, increased sensitivity to light
cornea infection.treatments: Antibiotic or antifungal eye drops, oral medications, supportive care, possible corneal transplant (in severe cases)

abscess of the lung.symptoms: Cough with phlegm or pus, chest pain, fever, shortness of breath, fatigue
abscess of the lung.treatments: Antibiotics, drainage or surgical removal of the abscess, supportive care, management of underlying lung conditions

dengue fever.symptoms: High fever, severe headache, joint and muscle pain, rash, nausea or vomiting, bleeding tendencies
dengue fever.treatments: Supportive care (hydration, pain relievers, rest), monitoring, medical attention for severe cases (fluid replacement, blood transfusion, hospitalization)

chronic sinusitis.symptoms: Facial pain or pressure, nasal congestion, thick nasal discharge, postnasal drip, cough, fatigue
chronic sinusitis.treatments: Saline nasal irrigation, nasal corticosteroids, antibiotics (in bacterial cases), decongestants, pain relievers, surgery (in severe or chronic cases)

cholesteatoma.symptoms: Ear pain, hearing loss, ear drainage, dizziness or vertigo, tinnitus

volvulus.symptoms: Abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, bloating, constipation, bloody stool
volvulus.treatments: Emergency surgery to untwist the affected organ and restore blood flow

injury to the finger.symptoms: Pain, swelling, bruising, deformity, restricted range of motion, difficulty gripping or using the finger
injury to the finger.treatments: RICE method (Rest, Ice, Compression, Elevation), splinting, pain medication, physical therapy, surgery (in severe cases)

poisoning due to analgesics.symptoms: Nausea, vomiting, abdominal pain, liver or kidney damage, dizziness, confusion, respiratory distress
poisoning due to analgesics.treatments: Immediate medical attention, activated charcoal administration, supportive care, antidote (if available), treatment for complications

atrial fibrillation.symptoms: Irregular or rapid heartbeat, palpitations, shortness of breath, chest pain or discomfort, dizziness or lightheadedness, fatigue
atrial fibrillation.treatments: Medications (antiarrhythmics, anticoagulants), electrical cardioversion, catheter ablation, lifestyle changes

pinworm infection.symptoms: Itching around the anus or vagina, restless sleep, irritability, anal or vaginal discharge, abdominal pain or discomfort, loss of appetite
pinworm infection.treatments: Anthelmintic medication, hygiene measures (handwashing, trimming nails, laundering bedding), treatment for household members

urethral valves.symptoms: Difficulty or poor urinary stream, urinary tract infections, urinary frequency or urgency, abdominal distension, poor weight gain
urethral valves.treatments: Surgical intervention (valve ablation or resection), catheterization, medications for symptoms and complications

migraine.symptoms: Severe headaches, nausea, sensitivity to light and sound
migraine.treatments: Pain-relieving medications, lifestyle changes, preventive medications

arthritis.treatments: Medications, physical therapy, surgery (in severe cases)

chronic headache.symptoms: Persistent head pain, tension in neck and shoulders
chronic headache.treatments: Pain-relieving medications, relaxation techniques, stress management

dry skin.treatments: Moisturizing creams or lotions, avoiding hot showers or baths, using mild soaps

eczema.treatments: Emollients (moisturizers), topical corticosteroids or immunomodulators, avoiding triggers

psoriasis.symptoms: Red, scaly patches on the skin, itching
psoriasis.treatments: Topical treatments (corticosteroids, salicylic acid), systemic medications (retinoids, immunosuppressants), phototherapy

scabies.symptoms: Intense itching, especially at night, small blisters or bumps
scabies.treatments: Prescription medications (topical or oral scabicides), washing clothes and bedding in hot water, vacuuming and cleaning home

urticaria (hives).symptoms: Raised, itchy welts on the skin
urticaria (hives).treatments: Antihistamines, avoiding triggers, corticosteroids (in severe cases)

insect bites.treatments: Topical corticosteroids, oral antihistamines, cold compresses

contact dermatitis.symptoms: Itchy, red rash caused by contact with irritants or allergens
contact dermatitis.treatments: Avoiding the trigger, corticosteroid creams or ointments, antihistamines

parasitic infections.symptoms: Intense itching, visible lice or nits in the affected area
parasitic infections.treatments: Prescription medications (topical or oral antiparasitic agents), washing clothes and bedding in hot water

influenza (flu).symptoms: High fever, body aches, fatigue, cough, sore throat, congestion
influenza (flu).treatments: Rest, fluids, over-the-counter medications for symptom relief, antiviral medications (in some cases)

bronchitis.symptoms: Persistent cough with yellow or green mucus, chest congestion, fatigue
bronchitis.treatments: Rest, fluids, over-the-counter cough suppressants, inhalers (if prescribed), treating underlying cause

gastroenteritis (stomach flu).symptoms: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, abdominal pain or cramps, fever
gastroenteritis (stomach flu).treatments: Rest, fluids (electrolyte solutions), bland diet, avoiding irritating foods, over-the-counter medications for symptom relief

urinary stones (kidney stones).symptoms: Severe abdominal or back pain, blood in urine, frequent urination, pain during urination
urinary stones (kidney stones).treatments: Pain management, increased fluid intake, medications to help pass the stones, procedures or surgery

osteoporosis.symptoms: Fragile bones, loss of height over time, back pain, fractures
osteoporosis.treatments: Calcium and vitamin D supplements, regular exercise, medications to slow bone loss

rheumatoid arthritis.symptoms: Joint pain, stiffness, swelling, fatigue, loss of appetite
rheumatoid arthritis.treatments: Medications (nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs, disease-modifying antirheumatic drugs), physical therapy, exercise

type 1 diabetes.symptoms: Frequent urination, Increased thirst, Weight loss
type 1 diabetes.treatments: Insulin therapy, Blood sugar monitoring, Healthy eating, Regular exercise

type 2 diabetes.symptoms: Fatigue, Increased hunger, Slow healing of wounds
type 2 diabetes.treatments: Oral medications, Insulin therapy (in some cases), Blood sugar monitoring, Exercise
